summary: Static verification of GitHub Actions workflow fix for build-android-apk.yml (setup-node@v4 strict-cache failure). All claims in review request verified. Backend regression suite re-run: 221 passed, 2 skipped (no regressions vs iter26 baseline of 216).

workflow_verification.setup_node_step_no_cache_options: PASS — only `node-version: '20'` under with:; no `cache:` or `cache-dependency-path:`
workflow_verification.cache_step_hashfiles_guard: PASS — `if: ${{ hashFiles('frontend/yarn.lock', 'yarn.lock') != '' }}` present on 'Cache yarn dependencies' step using actions/cache@v4
workflow_verification.yarn_install_fallback: PASS — 'Install frontend dependencies' step has `if [ -f yarn.lock ]; then yarn install --frozen-lockfile; else ... yarn install; fi` with ::warning:: annotation on fallback branch
workflow_verification.other_steps_unchanged: PASS — JDK 17, Android SDK, Preflight, vite build, cap add android, Gradle template apply, cap sync, gradlew assemble, locate APK, upload artifact, success summary all intact (15 steps total)
workflow_verification.original_failure_addressed: PASS — the exact error 'No existing directories found containing cache-dependency-path=frontend/yarn.lock' cannot occur anymore because cache-dependency-path directive is removed from setup-node; separate cache step is if-guarded and skips silently when no lockfile exists

critical_code_review_comments: Fix is canonical GitHub-recommended pattern (separate cache step + hashFiles guard). Clean and correct.
Minor: 'yarn install' fallback (without --frozen-lockfile) may drift lockfile silently; warning annotation appropriately flags this to user. Acceptable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter27 was a workflow-file-only fix. Backend baseline is now 221 passing tests. Workflow file at /app/.github/workflows/build-android-apk.yml correctly implements the setup-node cache workaround.
